_comment: CI regression baseline for the ws_idle_holder flagship demo (Phase 6.3 Slice 7). Consumed by bench_gate.py in the `bench-gate` job of .github/workflows/ci.yml. Numbers are the CI load tier (see `tier`), NOT the canonical 1M-scale rig figures in bench/REPORT.md — CI cannot hold 1M connections, so the gate runs a small steady-state exercise (2000 loopback conns + 3 churn rounds) that still catches density/latency/cliff regressions. `baseline` values were seeded from a real ubuntu-latest-class run (4 vCPU / 16 GB x86-64 Linux, LLVM 18.1.3 — the same class as GitHub's ubuntu-latest). Recalibrate on the actual runner with `bench_gate.py --update-baseline` if the first CI runs sit systematically off (the job is non-required until it builds a green streak, so a latency false-positive can't block anyone). Per-metric `tolerance_pct` is the allowed WORSENING before the gate fails; higher is worse for every tracked metric. The tight, deterministic sentinel is `memory.per_conn_bytes` (idle density is scale- & machine-invariant, ~1% run-to-run here); connect-latency percentiles are noisy on a shared runner so their tolerances are wide and catch only gross regressions. A metric with `baseline: null` is record-only. A regression is overridable via BENCH_GATE_OVERRIDE / a `[bench-override: <reason>]` commit-message token; correctness failures are never overridable.
